title: Ants Domesticated Fungi 30 Million Years Before We Did
script: Humans didn't invent farming — we just reinvented it 50 million years late. Leafcutter ants have been running full agriculture since around 60 million years ago. They grow a single fungus species underground, fertilize it with chewed leaves, and weed out competing molds. But here's the twist — that fungus evolved to ONLY survive in ant colonies. It can't exist without the ants. The ants can't survive without it. They domesticated each other. Half of you will refuse to believe a bug beat us to civilization.
search_summary: Leafcutter ants evolved fungus farming 60 million years ago, domesticating a fungus that cannot survive without them.
comment_question: If ants built farms before humans, what else are we NOT the first at? 🐜
source_note: Proceedings of the Royal Society B, Mueller et al., 2001
